MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Mar-14-2014 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Aug-01-2013 Continued To Apr-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Mtn To Strike The Fifth Cause Of Action From Complt
Off Calendar Apr-23-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No Appearance. Tentative ruling adopted as follows: DEFENDANT JOANNE DAKE's Motion To Strike The Fifth Cause Of Action From Complaint is DENIED. The only allegation in 5th causes of action that is potentially subject to CCP 425.16 is contained in paragraph 9f of Complaint incorporated by reference. 5th cause of action is additionally based on intentional failure to make repairs paragraph paragraph 21; abuses of right of access paragraph 22; threats and failure to maintain property paragraph 23, as well as other allegations in 9. "Where a cause of action arises from both protected activity and unprotected activity, the plaintiff may satisfy its obligation in the second prong by simply showing a probability of prevailing on any part of the cause of action." Wallace v. McCubbin (2011) 196 Cal.App.4th 1169, 1213. Keating provides uncontroverted evidence in her declaration regarding allegations in paragraph 21, 22, 23 and 9a-e, g. "We neither 'weight credibility, no compare the weight of the evidence. Rather, we accept as true the evidence favorable to the plaintiff and evaluate the defendant's evidence only to determine if it has defeated that submitted by the plaintiff as a matter of law." Oasis West Realty v. Goldman (2011) 51 Cal.4th 811, 820. Judge Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: M. Olopernes, Not reported (D501)
